title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot discusses the murder of a child named Joyce with Royana Drecker. Drecker suggests that the perpetrator is likely a teenager acting out of senseless destruction or revenge, arguing that such crimes often stem from difficult circumstances like broken homes. She believes the police share this view and advocates for remedial education over imprisonment.

Drecker recounts the death of her husband, who was paralyzed by polio six years ago and later killed in a traffic accident involving a stolen Volkswagen Beetle taken from a man named Mr. Watkinson in Manchester. She maintains the accident was manslaughter caused by irresponsible youth rather than premeditated murder. Poirot counters this by asserting that Joyce’s death was premeditated murder, noting that someone intentionally held the girl's head underwater. He suggests the motive was self-preservation, as Joyce had boasted shortly before her death that she had witnessed a killing.

Drecker suggests Poirot consult Miss Emmeline, the headmistress, for psychological insight into the students. Poirot also inquires about an exchange student who served as a companion to Drecker's aunt, Lady Llewellyn-Smith. Drecker reveals that the girl, who had been friendly with a lawyer in Manchester previously involved in a forgery case, disappeared after Lady Llewellyn-Smith's death. Drecker suspects the girl forged a codicil to her aunt's will. Following the conversation, Poirot walks to Helpsley Cemetery, a modern, tidy cemetery located near a small, centuries-old church.
